deck_goal: Help hackathon finalists build a short demo deck that judges can score quickly and fairly.

sessions.summary: Initial review checks that the deck does not claim a universal winning formula and keeps project-specific proof out of the generic eval.

slide_reviews.note: Rubric framing is sourced and caveated by the speaker notes.
Technical depth is framed as what to prove, not as an invented project result.
Close gives a retellable rule and invites critique of weak ideas.

recurring_issues: Do not imply one deck format guarantees a win.
Do not invent project-specific model, user, or benchmark results.
new_rules: Hackathon decks must record actual judging criteria before final slide selection.
Main-deck technical claims require project-specific proof before presentation.

sessions.summary: Manual screenshot review found that technically valid table slides still needed visual cleanup.

slide_reviews.note: Shortened the title and removed empty table background space so the rubric table reads cleaner.
Shortened the title to improve hierarchy and removed the transition ghost captured during screenshot review.

recurring_issues: A passing browser QA report does not guarantee the slide composition feels finished.
Avoid transitions in eval decks unless the QA screenshot loop explicitly needs to test motion.
new_rules: Spot-check screenshots after browser QA before calling an eval deck presentable.
Table themes should not paint unused empty background space.
